title: Proof-decomposition granularity as a training-data axis for small provers
description: Does the granularity (rough vs fine) at which a Lean/Mathlib proof is decomposed into sub-results change what a small prover learns? A formalism (sections of the proof DAG) + a machine-verified reconstruction pipeline + a planned SFT study. Reasoning reconstructed from the proof-hierarchies repo (branch iclr-foundation).

glossary.proof DAG: Directed acyclic graph over named Lean declarations; an edge d->e means d's elaborated proof term references constant e. Internal nodes are within-declaration have/suffices/show/calc/obtain steps.
glossary.expansion frontier (F): The set of declarations treated as opaque leaves when unfolding a proof; bounds the otherwise super-exponential unfolding. F=V recovers the in-proof have-tree; default unfold depth delta=1.
glossary.section: A maximal antichain (cut) of the bounded-unfolded proof poset: every root-to-leaf path meets it exactly once. THE decomposition - the independent variable of the study.
glossary.pi_root / pi_leaf (rough / fine): The two primary section policies: pi_root (rough) inlines intermediate haves and cites library lemmas; pi_leaf (fine) keeps haves named at the minimal antichain. They differ ~22% in reconstruction size.
glossary.generate-then-verify / yield: Reconstruct a decomposed proof, compile it against the original statement, and audit axioms (#print axioms subset of original); non-verifying reconstructions are dropped and counted as a reported yield (84.7%).
glossary.frozen stack: A single (Lean toolchain, Mathlib commit, repl) used identically across extraction, rewriting, verification, and eval - the biggest de-risking rule (zero extractor/verifier skew).
glossary.pass@k: The eval metric: fraction of held-out problems solved within k sampled generations, verified by the Kimina-Lean-Server.
glossary.granularity x model-size interaction: The core claimed effect: the rough-vs-fine held-out pass@k gap is hypothesized to GROW as the prover shrinks. Unmeasured.
glossary.Variant A / Variant B: Variant A (primary) = granularity as a training-data serialization axis; Variant B (deferred) = a section-predictor (subgoal frontier) + solver planner.
